# Noam Benjamin

> Bilingual (English/French) sales & business development profile from Montréal, QC, Canada. Founder of Afterlife Events, builder of TuneBad and BreakBuddy, self-taught mixing engineer, and Social Science student at Dawson College (DEC expected May 2026). Open to new opportunities.

This site (noambenjamin.com) is Noam Benjamin's CV and portfolio, available in 12 languages (en, fr, es, pt, de, it, zh, ja, ko, ru, ar, hi). Last updated: July 2026.

## Key facts

- Founder of Afterlife Events (July 2025–present): a Montréal events business built from scratch — 5 sold-out events, 1,500+ tickets sold, $50K+ revenue at ~47% profit margin in under one year, with zero ad spend. Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/afterlifevents/
- Built and shipped TuneBad (https://www.tunebad.com): a free, private, browser-based music toolkit for producers — BPM & key analysis, tempo-locked delay/reverb calculators, streaming loudness checks, slowed + reverb edits, and audio conversion, in 8 languages.
- Currently building BreakBuddy (https://breakbuddy-app.vercel.app): a social timetable app for college students that aligns class schedules with friends' so free periods overlap — see who's free right now and plan a break in two taps. Launched at Dawson College with app, API, and web live.
- Self-taught audio/mixing engineer, 6+ years: 600+ songs mixed for paying clients across pop, rap, Afrobeat and more (Logic Pro, FL Studio). 14 demo mixes are playable on the site.
- Skills: revenue growth, negotiation & closing, client relationships, outreach & prospecting, bilingual communication (native English + native French), lead generation & marketing.
- Education: Dawson College, Montréal — DEC in Social Science (expected May 2026); Villa Maria High School (2024).
